title: hoarea unguiculata
intro: Hoarea unguiculata, also known as horehound, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its expectorant properties, which help relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The medicinal properties of horehound include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce inflammation, and calm digestive issues. In herbal preparations, horehound is often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to capitalize on its health benefits, including reducing fever, improving respiratory function, and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu.
